name: La Foradada del Perelló - Circular by the Cardó-Boix mountain range
name_original: La Foradada del Perelló - Circular por la sierra de Cardó-Boix
region_intro: Hiking around El Perelló offers a diverse landscape that combines mountainous terrain with proximity to the Mediterranean coast and views of the Ebro Delta. The region features elevated trails in the Cardó-Boix mountain range, coastal paths with coves and beaches, and areas with unique geological formations. Historical elements like parts of the Via Augusta Roman road and the Torre dels Moros tower are integrated into the natural environment. The varied topography provides options for different hiking experiences, from gentle coastal walks to more challenging routes through hills. Best hiking trails around El Perelló The most popular hiking route is La Foradada del Perelló - Circular by the Cardó-Boix mountain range, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the mountainous landscape, offering elevated views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pont Aliga Beach – Cala de l'Estany Podrit loop from Platja del Pont de l'Àliga, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail follows the coastline, passing beaches and coves. Local hikers also love the Tierras Blancas Trail loop from el Perelló, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through distinctive "white lands" terrain, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around El Perelló is defined by mountainous areas, coastal paths, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate ascents. The routes in El Perelló are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore El Perelló's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around El Perelló?
A: El Perelló offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 29 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different preferences, ranging from easy coastal walks to more challenging mountainous ascents.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in El Perelló?
A: Hiking in El Perelló provides a unique blend of landscapes. You'll find trails winding through the mountainous terrain of the Cardó-Boix range, offering elevated views, as well as coastal paths with peaceful beaches and impressive cliffs. The area also boasts unique geological formations and offers panoramic vistas overlooking the Ebro Delta.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in El Perelló?
A: Yes, El Perelló has plenty of easy trails perfect for beginners or families. About half of the routes, 15 out of 29, are rated as easy. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap Roig Beach – Cala Maria loop from Platja de Perales&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.1 miles (6.6 km) long and takes about 2 hours to complete, offering beautiful coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Perelló?
A: Absolutely, many of the trails in El Perelló are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e953725290/la-foradada-del-perello-circular-by-the-cardo-boix-mountain-range" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada del Perelló - Circular by the Cardó-Boix mountain range&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.3-mile (10.1 km) route that explores the mountainous landscape. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierras Blancas Trail loop from el Perelló&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.4-mile (7.1 km) trail through distinctive terrain.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: El Perelló's trails are rich in history. You can walk along parts of the ancient Via Augusta Roman Road or visit the 19th-century Torre dels Moros tower, also known as Torre de les Guàrdies. For natural and historical interest, the Cabra-Feixet rock paintings, a UNESCO World Heritage site, are also in the area. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fangar Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llop Marí Cove and Cave&lt;/a&gt; near some coastal routes.

Q: Are there hikes with good viewpoints in El Perelló?
A: Yes, El Perelló is known as the 'Delta Viewpoint' due to its privileged position. Many trails offer stunning vistas, especially those in the mountainous areas. The 'La Roca Blanca' trail is particularly recommended for breathtaking aerial views. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3427646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ebro Valley from Coll de Miravet&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in El Perelló?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, which seamlessly blends mountains with the Mediterranean coast, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels. The unique geological formations and historical elements also add to the appeal.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Perelló?
A: El Perelló is an excellent destination for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for exploring both coastal and mountainous routes. Even in winter, the climate is generally mild, making it suitable for hiking. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season, especially for coastal paths.

Q: Are there any coastal hiking trails in El Perelló?
A: Yes, El Perelló offers beautiful coastal hiking paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Aliga Beach – Cala de l'Estany Podrit loop from Platja del Pont de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 6.2-mile (10.0 km) trail that follows the coastline, passing by various beaches and coves. These routes provide stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Is it possible to access hiking trails in El Perelló using public transport?
A: While many trails start directly from El Perelló village, which is accessible by public transport, some more remote trailheads might require a short taxi ride or personal vehicle. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if you plan to rely solely on public transport for specific starting points outside the village center.

Q: Are there any specific natural features or unique spots to explore?
A: Beyond the general landscapes, El Perelló features several unique natural spots. You can explore areas like 'La Font de Tita' for a tranquil connection with nature, or visit 'El Pi de l'Agna' to see a large, unusually shaped pine tree and indigenous flora. The region also has ponds and natural basins such as 'La Bassa de les Comes' and 'La Bassa de l'Espinal', which are great for observing local flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous experience, El Perelló offers several moderate to challenging routes. While there are no trails classified as 'difficult' in the komoot guide, the 14 moderate routes provide a good challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e953725290/la-foradada-del-perello-circular-by-the-cardo-boix-mountain-range" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada del Perelló - Circular by the Cardó-Boix mountain range&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, involves significant elevation gain over its 6.3 miles (10.1 km) and takes approximately 3 hours 20 minutes.
tips: The Cabrafeixet Cave or Cabrafreixet Shelter is a cave with rock painting representations protected as a World Heritage Site in the group of rock art of the Mediterranean arc of the Iberian Peninsula. There are 13 representations.
The Morral de Cabrafeixet is one of the highest mountains in Les Moles, within the mountain system of the Serra del Boix.

Beginning of the top course, well prepared path and visible indication. But once you get to the first pass… Morral de Cabrafeixet… it gets worse the further you go, the more the path disappears, same for the markings. We have to climb between the rocks… Really not accessible to anyone!
In addition, the wearing of trousers is strongly recommended. The narrow path lined with thorny plants that lacerate your calves…. Really not great.
